title: Force autoplay background audio at 60% volume with clown-laugh fallback and no controls

requirements.summary: Force chainsaw loop autoplay at 0.6 volume and remove all audio controls/prompts from the UI
acceptanceCriteria: The chainsaw audio element uses the existing file path "/assets/audio/chainsaw-loop.mp3" and loops continuously.
The chainsaw audio volume is set to 0.6.
On page load, the app attempts to start playback automatically without requiring the user to click a sound button.
No sound on/off toggle button is rendered anywhere (including the fixed bottom-right icon button).
No "Enable Sound" prompt UI is shown (including the fixed top-right prompt card).

file_operations.description: Update background audio to always attempt autoplay of the chainsaw loop on mount, set chainsaw volume to 0.6 and loop=true, and remove all sound UI (including the fixed toggle button and any "Enable sound" prompt rendering/logic) while keeping the component mounted in the app.

requirements.summary: Add an automatic looping clown-laugh audio fallback when chainsaw autoplay fails (no controls)
acceptanceCriteria: A new static audio asset exists at "/assets/audio/clown-laugh.mp3" (or equivalent under frontend/public/assets/audio so it is reachable at that URL).
If the initial chainsaw autoplay attempt fails/throws, the app attempts to autoplay the clown-laugh audio automatically as a fallback.
The clown-laugh fallback loops continuously when it is the active audio.
The fallback logic does not introduce new UI controls/buttons for audio.

file_operations.description: Add the new static audio file so it is reachable at the URL "/assets/audio/clown-laugh.mp3".

Extend the autoplay logic: if chainsaw.play() fails/throws, automatically attempt to start a looping clown-laugh Audio instance (using "/assets/audio/clown-laugh.mp3") as a fallback, without rendering any user-facing audio controls.

requirements.summary: Mitigate browser autoplay restrictions by re-attempting playback on first-user-gesture events without adding audio UI
acceptanceCriteria: When autoplay is blocked on initial load, the app registers first-user-gesture listeners and automatically re-attempts playback once a gesture occurs (without showing a dedicated audio prompt UI).
After playback successfully starts once, the gesture listeners are cleaned up to avoid repeated calls.
No additional audio-related UI is added as part of this mitigation.

file_operations.description: After an initial autoplay failure, register best-effort first-user-gesture listeners (e.g., pointerdown/click/touchstart/keydown on window/document) that re-attempt starting the chainsaw loop first, then the clown-laugh fallback if needed; once playback starts successfully, remove the listeners and avoid repeated play attempts, while keeping the experience control-free.
